title: astraea surinamensis
intro: Astraea surinamensis, also known as the "Surinam Queen's Crest" or "Queen's Crest", is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is a shrub native to the tropical forests of South America, particularly in Suriname and Guyana, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. Astraea surinamensis is often prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
